title: Using AI for Plugin Development
description: Learn how to properly use AI step-by-step to help you code, debug, and learn without becoming dependent.

lessons.title: Why Use AI?
lessons.content:
# Why Use AI?

## Step 1: Understand When You Are Stuck
When developing plugins, you will eventually:
- Get errors
- Not understand something
- Be unsure what to do next

This is NORMAL.

## Step 2: What AI Actually Does
AI tools (like ChatGPT) do NOT magically code for you.
They:
- Explain things in simpler terms
- Help you understand errors
- Suggest possible fixes

Think of AI like a teacher sitting next to you.

## Step 3: Real Workflow (IMPORTANT)
Follow this EXACT process:

1. Try solving the problem yourself first
2. If stuck for a while → use AI
3. Read the answer carefully
4. Try it yourself

## Example (Real Situation)
You run your plugin and the server crashes.

Instead of panicking:

Step 1: Open your server console
Step 2: Find the red error text
Step 3: Copy the FULL error
Step 4: Ask AI:
"What does this error mean?"

## Why This Matters
If you skip trying yourself:
- You won’t learn
- You’ll rely on AI forever

## Simple Analogy
AI = Calculator
You = Brain

Calculator helps, but you still need to understand maths.

## Key Rule
AI is a TOOL, not your brain.

quiz.question: What is AI best used for?

lessons.title: Popular AI Tools
lessons.content:
# AI Tools You Can Use

## Step 1: Open Your Browser
You will mainly use AI in your browser while coding.

## Step 2: Main Tools

### ChatGPT
- Best for explanations
- Great for debugging errors
- Can explain code line-by-line

### Google AI Studio
- Good for testing ideas
- Can compare different prompts

### GitHub Copilot
- Works INSIDE your code editor (like IntelliJ)
- Suggests code as you type

### Claude
- Good for longer explanations

## Step 3: Where You Use Them

While coding your plugin:
- IntelliJ = writing code
- Browser = asking AI questions

You switch between them constantly.

## Important Concept
Different tools are better at different things.

If one AI gives a bad answer:
→ Try another one

## Example Workflow
1. Code in IntelliJ
2. Error happens
3. Switch to browser
4. Ask AI
5. Go back and fix code

## Tip
Do NOT rely on just one AI forever.

## Why This Matters
Some AIs:
- Explain better
- Some code better
- Some debug better

quiz.question: What should you do if one AI fails?

lessons.title: How to Ask AI Properly
lessons.content:
# How to Ask AI Properly

## Step 1: Understand Why Prompts Matter
AI is not magic.

If you give a bad question → you get a bad answer.

## Step 2: Bad vs Good Prompt

### ❌ Bad Prompt
"Fix my code"

This is bad because:
- No context
- No code
- No error

### ✅ Good Prompt
"My Minecraft plugin crashes when I break a block. I get a NullPointerException. Here is my code: ..."

## Step 3: What You MUST Include

Always include:

1. What you are trying to do
2. The error message (copy from console)
3. Your code
4. What you expected to happen

## Step 4: Where to Get Errors

In your server console:
- Look for RED text
- That is your error

Copy EVERYTHING (don’t cut it)

## Step 5: Example Full Prompt

"I am making a Minecraft plugin. When I run /heal, it crashes. Here is the error: ... Here is my command class: ... I expected it to heal the player."

## Why This Works
The AI now has:
- Context
- Code
- Error

So it can actually help.

## Beginner Mistake
❌ Asking vague questions

## Fix
✅ Always be specific

lessons.title: Don’t Overuse AI
lessons.content:
# Don’t Overuse AI

## Step 1: Understand the Problem
If you use AI for EVERYTHING:
- Your brain stops learning
- You become dependent

## Step 2: Bad Habit
Copy-pasting code without reading it

Example:
- You paste AI code
- It breaks later
- You have NO idea why

## Step 3: Good Habit
ALWAYS do this:

1. Read the code
2. Try to understand each line
3. Ask AI: "Explain this line"
4. Modify it yourself

## Step 4: The Rule
If you don’t understand it → DO NOT USE IT YET

## Analogy
Using AI without understanding = copying homework

You finish it...
But you learned nothing.

## Step 5: Smart Use

Good developer:
- Uses AI to learn

Bad developer:
- Uses AI to skip thinking

## Final Rule
AI should make you smarter, not lazier

quiz.question: What happens if you overuse AI?

lessons.title: Real Example: Fixing a Bug
lessons.content:
# Real Example: Fixing a Bug

## Situation
Your plugin command crashes the server.

## Step-by-Step Fix

### Step 1: Run Your Server
Start your Minecraft server normally.

### Step 2: Trigger the Problem
Run your command (e.g. /heal)

### Step 3: Open Console
Look at your server console.

### Step 4: Find the Error
- Look for RED text
- This is the error

### Step 5: Copy the Error
Select ALL of it

### Step 6: Ask AI
Paste it and ask:
"Why is this error happening?"

### Step 7: Read Carefully
Don’t just scroll.
Understand what it says.

### Step 8: Fix Your Code
Go back to IntelliJ:
- Open the file mentioned in the error
- Find the line number
- Fix it

### Step 9: Test Again
Restart server
Run command again

## Important Rule
ALWAYS test after using AI

## Common Mistake
Fixing code without testing

## Result
You:
- Fix the bug
- Understand the problem
- Improve your skills

lessons.title: Best Practices
lessons.content:
# Best Practices

## Step 1: Follow This Workflow

1. Try yourself
2. Get stuck
3. Use AI
4. Understand answer
5. Apply it
6. Test

## Step 2: Golden Rules

- Never blindly copy code
- Always understand what you paste
- Always test your plugin

## Step 3: Developer Mindset

Good developers:
- Ask WHY
- Experiment
- Learn from mistakes

## Step 4: Final Rule (MOST IMPORTANT)

AI should HELP you think
NOT replace thinking

## Recap

AI is powerful
But only if YOU are learning

If you rely on it too much:
You will get stuck later

If you use it properly:
You will improve FAST
